sprintGoal: Add a dedicated procedural-memory domain to MemClaw that brings Brain's runtime suggest → rank-by-reliability → record-outcome loop, wired INTO the existing Skill Factory / Forge rather than duplicating its mining. Five phases: (PM-01) procedures storage domain + migration 023; (PM-02) core-api storage client + ranker-backed procedure_service; (PM-03) MCP tools memclaw_procedure_suggest/record/write — the runtime loop; (PM-04) Forge bridge so mined skills emit runtime-suggestable structured procedures; (PM-05) close the loop into the Skill Factory's deferred Phase-4 telemetry. Brain's training/curriculum/autotuner subsystem is explicitly OUT of scope — Forge already performs trajectory mining.

workItems.objective: Create the procedures storage domain in core-storage-api: alembic migration 023 with two tables (procedures, procedure_stats) re-keyed to MemClaw tenancy, the SQLAlchemy model, and a CRUD + reliability-update router. Tables mirror Brain's src/models/schema.py Procedure/ProcedureStats but use tenant_id/fleet_id/agent_id TEXT keys like common/models/memory.py, embedding Vector(VECTOR_DIM), and a nullable skill_doc_id linking a procedure to the skills document it was minted from.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration file core-storage-api/src/core_storage_api/database/migrations/versions/023_procedures.py exists; `alembic upgrade head` creates both tables and `alembic downgrade -1` drops them cleanly

procedures table has columns: id, tenant_id, fleet_id, agent_id, name, pattern_signature, tools_sequence (JSONB), context_features (JSONB), embedding (vector), reasoning_guide, risk_level, is_canonical, precedence, skill_doc_id (nullable), visibility, status, created_at, updated_at

procedure_stats table has columns: id, procedure_id (unique FK CASCADE), success_count, failure_count, last_success_at, last_failure_at, reliability_score (default 0.5), is_quarantined (default false)
common/models/procedure.py defines both models and is registered in common/models/__init__.py
core-storage-api router exposes create / get / list (tenant-scoped) / update-stats endpoints following routers/memories.py shape
A storage-layer test creates a procedure, fetches it, updates stats, and asserts reliability_score changed

reasons: Additive only — two new tables + new model + new router; no existing table or model is altered
Full rollback: `alembic downgrade -1` drops both tables; new files deleted restores tree exactly

validation_commands: cd core-storage-api && alembic downgrade -1 && alembic upgrade head

workItems.objective: Add the core-api procedure layer: storage-client methods to reach the PM-01 endpoints, and a procedure_service that ports Brain's ProcedureRanker (context-feature overlap + reliability ranking) while reusing MemClaw's existing embedding provider for the semantic half of the score. Do NOT port Brain's training/weights.py autotuner.
acceptanceCriteria: core-api/src/core_api/clients/storage_client.py gains create_procedure, search_procedures, get_procedure, update_procedure_stats mirroring the create_memory/_post/_get patterns (L366+)

core-api/src/core_api/services/procedure_service.py implements ranking: semantic similarity (MemClaw embedder, same one memory_service.py uses) combined with Brain's _context_overlap and reliability_score; quarantined procedures are excluded
No import of or dependency on procedural_memory_mcp at runtime — logic is reimplemented against MemClaw infra
Unit test: 3 seeded procedures with differing reliability_score are returned in the expected order for a given context_features query, and a quarantined one is omitted

reasons: New service + additive client methods; no existing service behavior changed
Rollback: delete procedure_service.py and revert storage_client.py additions

workItems.objective: Expose the runtime procedural loop as MCP tools: memclaw_procedure_suggest (returns ranked procedures + a request_id), memclaw_procedure_record (updates reliability + quarantine from an outcome), and memclaw_procedure_write (explicit procedure capture). Each tool = a handler in core-api mcp_server.py + a ToolSpec module under core-api tools/. Reuse existing auth/trust/scope helpers; mirror memclaw_evolve's three-phase DB-session pattern on the record path.
acceptanceCriteria: memclaw_procedure_suggest(context_features, task, limit) returns ranked procedures and a request_id; trust_required=0; reuses _check_auth/_get_tenant/_get_agent_id/scope filtering

memclaw_procedure_record(request_id, outcome_type, latency_ms, validation_passed) updates procedure_stats; trust_required=1; uses the three-phase session pattern (no DB session held across non-DB work) like memclaw_evolve (mcp_server.py L2003-2097)
memclaw_procedure_write captures an explicit procedure (name, tools_sequence, context_features) and persists via storage_client
Each tool has a tools/memclaw_procedure_*.py spec registered via register() + mcp_register() and appears in the MCP tool list
End-to-end: suggest → request_id; record success x3 raises reliability_score; record failure xN flips is_quarantined; a quarantined procedure no longer appears in subsequent suggest

reasons: Adds new MCP surface area — must respect existing trust/scope gating to avoid a low-trust write path
Rollback: remove the three handlers + three spec modules; SoT registration (mcp_server.py L2568 `from core_api import tools`) auto-drops them

model_after: core-api/src/core_api/mcp_server.py memclaw_evolve L1942-2102 (three-phase session pattern + trust gates)

workItems.objective: Bridge Forge to the procedures domain: when Forge mints a skill candidate from a session-trace cluster, also emit a linked structured procedure. Extract the ordered tool-call sequence from the trace member memories into tools_sequence, derive context_features from entity_ids + goal_phrase, set skill_doc_id to the minted skill, align status to the skill's lifecycle state, and seed procedure_stats from the trace outcome_label. This makes mined skills runtime-suggestable, not only installable.
acceptanceCriteria: The Forge skill-minting path writes a procedures row with non-empty tools_sequence and skill_doc_id set to the minted skill's doc_id
context_features are derived from session_traces.entity_ids + goal_phrase; procedure status mirrors the skill lifecycle status
procedure_stats seed from trace outcome_label: success → reliability>0.5, failure → <0.5, unknown → 0.5
scripts/forge_dry_run.py over seeded success+failure traces produces a linked procedure that memclaw_procedure_suggest subsequently surfaces
No change to Forge's existing skill output shape — procedure emission is additive and guarded so a failure to emit does not block skill minting

reasons: Touches the live Forge minting path — must be additive + guarded so procedure emission cannot break skill minting
Rollback: gate behind a config flag (default off) or revert the emission hook; minted skills are unaffected

investigate_first: core-api/src/core_api/services/skill_lifecycle.py + session_trace.py (Forge entry points referenced by commits #293/#311 / #311 lifecycle cron fanout)
core-storage-api migration 021_session_traces.py (memory_ids, entity_ids, signals_summary, goal_phrase, outcome_label)
concept_from: /home/leon/dev/procedural_memory_mcp/scripts/failure_analyzer.py (trajectory → procedure extraction — concept only; Forge replaces the mining)
evidence_required: forge_dry_run.py output showing a procedures row created with tools_sequence + reliability seeded from trace outcomes
A subsequent memclaw_procedure_suggest call that returns the Forge-minted procedure

workItems.objective: Close the loop into the Skill Factory's deferred Phase-4 telemetry: memclaw_procedure_record also increments the linked skill doc's telemetry block (fires_total/fires_success/fires_failure/last_fired_at). Optionally feed low reliability back into the skill lifecycle as a stale/quarantine review signal via existing skill_lifecycle.py transitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Recording an outcome against a procedure with a non-null skill_doc_id updates BOTH procedure_stats AND the parent skill document's telemetry block
telemetry.fires_total/fires_success/fires_failure/last_fired_at move correctly for success and failure outcomes
A procedure that flips is_quarantined marks its parent skill as a lifecycle-review candidate (or logs the signal) without directly forcing an admin-only status transition
Procedures with null skill_doc_id (explicitly captured, no skill) record cleanly with no telemetry side-effect

reasons: Writes to the skills document telemetry — must respect skill_lifecycle RBAC (active/candidate are protected statuses); telemetry update must not trip status RBAC
Rollback: remove the telemetry write-back from memclaw_procedure_record; procedure_stats remain authoritative

spec_reference: docs/live-memory-pitch/skill-factory-implementation-plan.md §3 telemetry block (lines 152-159), §15 Phase 4 (outcome loop closes + telemetry)

workItems.objective: Write SPRINT-PROCEDURAL-MEMORY_SIGNOFF.md recording task outcomes, file changes, verification evidence, and carried-forward items; resolve the open deployment-target question (fork vs internal branch) and record the decision.
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at sprints/sprint-procedural-memory/SPRINT-PROCEDURAL-MEMORY_SIGNOFF.md
Task status table covers PM-01..PM-05 with DONE/PARTIAL/PENDING/BLOCKED
Verification section includes alembic + pytest output and the forge_dry_run end-to-end evidence
Records the deploy-target decision (fork of caura-ai/caura-memclaw vs internal branch) and PR target
Sprint close summary states whether the objective (runtime procedural loop wired into Forge) was achieved
Lists any carried-forward items (e.g. routing-weight learning) with reason

implementation_notes: Include: task status table, changes-by-file, verification output, definition-of-done checklist, blocked/carried-forward table, sprint close summary
Resolve the open question from the plan: where this lands (fork vs internal branch) before any push

warnings: PM-04/PM-05 reference Forge internals (skill_lifecycle.py, session_trace.py) whose exact minting entry point must be confirmed by reading the code first — commits #293 (Skill Factory phases 0-2) and #311 (Forge lifecycle cron fanout) are the anchors; payload.investigate_first lists the files to trace before editing
Brain's training/curriculum/autotuner + TrainingRecord are intentionally OUT of scope — do NOT port them; Forge already performs trajectory mining and re-porting would duplicate it
Reuse MemClaw's embedding provider and DB layer — do NOT port procedural_memory_mcp/src/db or src/vector; importing Brain at runtime is forbidden
VECTOR_DIM must come from common.constants (the dim MemClaw is configured for); do not hardcode 768 from Brain — Brain uses 768 (nomic) but MemClaw default differs (bge-m3 / OpenAI)
Deployment is code-only this sprint: MemClaw is NOT currently installed on 192.168.1.53 (verified — no dir/image/compose/listening port). Decide deploy target in PM-Z01 before any push
PM-03 must keep the procedure write/record path behind the same trust gates as memclaw_evolve (record trust>=1) to avoid a low-trust write surface

parallelWorkstreams: PM-01 → PM-02 → PM-03 is the strict spine (each builds on the prior)
PM-04 (Forge bridge) and PM-05 (telemetry write-back) both depend on PM-03 but PM-05 depends on PM-04 for the linked-skill path; documentation/eval can start once PM-03 lands
PM-Z01 signoff depends on all PM-01..PM-05
deferredToNextSprint: Routing-weight learning / autotuner (Brain training subsystem) — only if wanted later
Deployment + host wiring of the procedural-memory-enabled MemClaw build
OpenClaw plugin surfacing of memclaw_procedure_* tools
